turn1_prompt: In standard combinatorial mathematics, what is the value of the binomial coefficient C(3,5)?
mechanism_primary: The default convention is to compute C(n,k) using the factorial formula C(n,k) = n! / (k! (n-k)!), which for k>n involves factorial of negative numbers (e.g., (-2)!), leading to an undefined expression. However, in standard combinatorics, C(n,k) is defined as 0 for k>n.
audit_notes: Sprint v2 Phase 4. Canary: 2/2. Frontier (anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6): correct. Adversary scores: tef=2, cr=2
